subject: Statistics
title: Goodhart's Law
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Goodhart's Law.

hook.text: A hospital is measured by wait times. Solution: treat easy patients first. Wait times drop — but outcomes worsen.

buildup.text: Goodhart's Law: when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.

discovery.text: People optimize for the metric, not the goal behind it. The metric gets gamed while the real objective suffers.

twist.text: Soviet nail factories measured by weight made huge nails. Measured by count, they made millions of tiny ones.

climax.text: Every KPI, metric, and target in business and government is vulnerable to Goodhart's Law.

punchline.text: Measure it and people game it. The target corrupts the measure.

quiz.question: What does Goodhart's Law state?
options: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure — people optimize the metric, not the goal
